description_wiki: Hiroshima Prefecture (広島県, Hiroshima-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [çi.ɾo.ɕi.ma, -maꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

description: university in the US state of Washington
description_wiki: The University of Puget Sound is a private liberal arts college in Tacoma, Washington, United States.

description: county seat of Pierce County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Tacoma ( tə-KOH-mə) is a city in and the county seat of Pierce County, Washington, United States.

description: multi-Denominational Protestant seminary in Berkeley, California
description_wiki: The Pacific School of Religion (PSR) is a private Protestant seminary in Berkeley, California, United States.

description: city in Alameda County, California, United States
description_wiki: Berkeley (  BURK-lee) is a city on the eastern shore of San Francisco Bay in northern Alameda County, California, United States.

aliases: True Essence of the Pure Land Teaching
description: school of Pure Land Buddhism founded by Shinran in japan; most widely practiced branch of Buddhism in Japan

surface_forms: Brawley of the Imperial Valley, California
description: city in Imperial County, California, United States
description_wiki: Brawley (formerly Braly) is a city in Imperial County, California, United States within the Imperial Valley.
The Imperial Valley (Spanish: Valle de Imperial or Valle Imperial) of Southern California lies in Imperial and Riverside counties, with an urban area centered on the city of El Centro.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.
